question: Ethereum Up or Down - February 5, 11:15AM-11:30AM ET
description:

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the ETH/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/eth-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream ETH/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

This market will resolve to "Karmine Corp GC" if Karmine Corp GC wins 2 or more maps than G2 Gozen in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "G2 Gozen".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - February 6, 12PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Open Sud de France: Luca Nardi vs Flavio Cobolli
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Luca Nardi and Flavio Cobolli in the Open Sud de France, scheduled for February 5 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Nardi' if Luca Nardi advances against Flavio Cobolli.

This market will resolve to 'Cobolli' if Flavio Cobolli advances against Luca Nardi.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Transylvania Open: Yue Yuan vs Daria Snigur
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Yue Yuan and Daria Snigur in the Transylvania Open, scheduled for February 5 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Yuan' if Yue Yuan advances against Daria Snigur.

This market will resolve to 'Snigur' if Daria Snigur advances against Yue Yuan.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Ostrava Open: Linda Fruhvirtova vs Katie Boulter
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Linda Fruhvirtova and Katie Boulter in the Ostrava Open, scheduled for February 6 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Fruhvirtova' if Linda Fruhvirtova advances against Katie Boulter.

This market will resolve to 'Boulter' if Katie Boulter advances against Linda Fruhvirtova.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 48.5 in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming match between GamerLegion and Tundra Esports in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 11:35AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 49 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

events.description:

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

This market will resolve to "GamerLegion" if GamerLegion win the match against Tundra Esports.

This market will resolve to "Tundra Esports" if Tundra Esports win the match against GamerLegion.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - February 6, 1PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Fruhvirtova vs. Boulter: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Linda Fruhvirtova and Katie Boulter in the Ostrava Open, scheduled for February 6 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA results.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - February 6, 1PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the ETH/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Blackhawks vs. Blue Jackets: O/U 5.5
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for February 4 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Blackhawks and Blue Jackets combine to score 6 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 6, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

question: U19 World Cup: England Under-19s vs India Under-19s
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between England Under-19s and India Under-19s scheduled for February 6 2026 in U19 World Cup.
This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  
DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.
If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between England Under-19s and India Under-19s scheduled for 2026-02-06 is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.

description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between England Under-19s and India Under-19s scheduled for 2026-02-06.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to England Under-19s will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from England Under-19s.
The outcome corresponding to India Under-19s will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from India Under-19s.

question: WPL: Royal Challengers Bengaluru Women vs Delhi Capitals Women - Most Sixes Royal Challengers Bengaluru Women Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Royal Challengers Bengaluru Women and Delhi Capitals Women scheduled for 2026-02-05.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Royal Challengers Bengaluru Women will be considered correct if Royal Challengers Bengaluru Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Delhi Capitals Women.The outcome corresponding to Delhi Capitals Women will be considered correct if Delhi Capitals Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Royal Challengers Bengaluru Women.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 52.5 in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming match between Natus Vincere and Team Yandex in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 1:45PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 53 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

events.title: Dota 2: Natus Vincere vs Team Yandex (BO1) - BLAST Slam Group Stage
events.description:

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - February 5, 2:15PM-2:30PM ET
description:

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

This market will resolve to "Up" if the XRP price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the XRP/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/xrp-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream XRP/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 36.5 in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming match between Natus Vincere and Team Yandex in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 1:45PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 37 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

events.title: Dota 2: Natus Vincere vs Team Yandex (BO1) - BLAST Slam Group Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Natus Vincere and Team Yandex in the BLAST Slam Group Stage, scheduled for February 4 at 12:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against Team Yandex.

This market will resolve to "Team Yandex" if Team Yandex win the match against Natus Vincere.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.liquipedia.net/dota2/Main_Page.
